openmeter/billing/models/creditsapplied.purpose: Pure domain model package defining CreditsApplied (named slice) and CreditApplied (value type) representing credits applied to billing line items — enforces a positive-amount invariant and currency-precision-aware summation. No persistence code lives here.

patterns.name: models.Validator compile-time assertion
patterns.description: Every type implements models.Validator via Validate() error and is verified with var _ models.Validator = (*T)(nil).

patterns.name: models.Clonable deep-copy via lo.Map
patterns.description: CreditsApplied implements models.Clonable[CreditsApplied] via lo.Map; new slice/pointer fields must be deep-copied in Clone.
patterns.example: func (c CreditsApplied) Clone() CreditsApplied { return lo.Map(c, func(item CreditApplied, _ int) CreditApplied { return item }) }

patterns.name: Currency-aware summation with RoundToPrecision
patterns.description: SumAmount rounds each item via currencyx.Calculator.RoundToPrecision before accumulating; new aggregation helpers must use the calculator.

patterns.name: Positive-amount enforcement in Validate
patterns.description: CreditApplied.Validate() rejects non-positive amounts; never accumulate credits without validating.
patterns.example: if !c.Amount.IsPositive() { return errors.New("amount must be positive") }

key_file_guides.role: Single source of CreditsApplied, CreditApplied and all methods (Clone, Validate, SumAmount, CloneWithAmount).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Amount must stay positive (validated). Adding nullable fields requires updating Clone and Validate. No DB/Ent imports here.

anti_patterns: Storing negative credit amounts — Validate() enforces positivity.
Accumulating amounts without RoundToPrecision — causes currency precision drift.
Adding DB/Ent concerns — this is a pure domain model.
Using == instead of .Equal() to compare alpacadecimal.Decimal fields.

decisions.decision: CreditsApplied is a named slice type rather than a struct with a slice field.
decisions.rationale: Allows direct iteration, len checks, and method attachment without wrapping, matching other billing model collections.

openmeter/billing/models/externalid.purpose: Defines external-ID domain structs (InvoiceExternalIDs, LineExternalIDs) and generic Ent mixin/helper functions for reading, writing, and mapping external app IDs (invoicing, payment) on invoice and line entities — bridging domain model and Ent schema without coupling either side.

patterns.name: Generic Creator/Updater/Getter interfaces per entity
patterns.description: Each CRUD op has a typed generic interface; adapters call package helpers (CreateLineExternalID, UpdateLineExternalID, MapLineExternalIDFromDB) rather than Set* methods directly.
patterns.example: func CreateLineExternalID[T LineExternalIDCreator[T]](creator LineExternalIDCreator[T], ids LineExternalIDs) T { return creator.SetNillableInvoicingAppExternalID(lo.EmptyableToPtr(ids.Invoicing)) }

patterns.name: lo.EmptyableToPtr for optional strings
patterns.description: Empty strings become nil pointers when writing nullable Ent columns; new external-ID fields must follow this.

patterns.name: Ent mixin for schema field declarations
patterns.description: LineMixin and InvoiceMixin declare optional/nillable string fields; new fields go in the correct mixin plus the typed interfaces.

patterns.name: Lockstep field addition across four locations
patterns.description: A new external-ID field requires updating Mixin Fields(), Creator/Updater/Getter interfaces, Create/Update/Map helpers, and the model.go struct (incl. Equal).
patterns.example: // mixin.go InvoiceMixin + InvoiceExternalIDCreator[T] + CreateInvoiceExternalID + InvoiceExternalIDs struct

key_file_guides.role: Ent mixin schema definitions and generic CRUD helpers for invoice and line external IDs.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New field requires updating all four locations: Mixin Fields(), Creator/Updater/Getter interfaces, Create/Update/Map helpers, model.go struct.

key_file_guides.role: Pure domain structs InvoiceExternalIDs and LineExternalIDs with nil-safe accessors and LineExternalIDs.Equal.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Update LineExternalIDs.Equal when new fields are added. GetInvoicingOrEmpty uses a nil-receiver guard — replicate for new accessors.

anti_patterns: Storing empty strings in nullable external-ID columns — use lo.EmptyableToPtr.
Calling Set* Ent methods directly in adapters instead of the typed helpers.
Adding business logic or validation here — it is a model/mixin utility layer.
Forgetting to update LineExternalIDs.Equal when adding a new string field.

decisions.decision: Generic interfaces (Creator[T], Updater[T], Getter) rather than concrete Ent types.
decisions.rationale: Both invoice and line entities share the same external-ID fields; generics let one helper work across both without duplication.

openmeter/billing/models/stddetailedline.purpose: Defines the Base struct for standard detailed billing lines (flat-fee and usage-based), the Ent mixin providing all DB columns, generic Creator/DBGetter interfaces for Ent mutations and mapping, and goderive-generated equality — the shared schema/model layer consumed by both Ent adapters and domain services.

patterns.name: Composite Ent mixin via entutils.RecursiveMixin
patterns.description: Mixin embeds entutils.RecursiveMixin[mixinBase] composing AnnotationsMixin, ResourceMixin, and totals.Mixin. New fields go in mixinBase.Fields(), not entity schemas.

patterns.name: Generic Creator[T] for Ent create mutations
patterns.description: Creator[T] composes externalid.LineExternalIDCreator[T], billingtotals.Setter[T], and primitive Set* methods; Create[T] atomically populates all fields.
patterns.example: func Create[T Creator[T]](creator Creator[T], line Base) T

patterns.name: DBGetter for DB-to-domain mapping
patterns.description: FromDB[T DBGetter] reads all fields via the getter interface; all time.Time fields normalize to UTC via .In(time.UTC).
patterns.example: func FromDB[T DBGetter](dbEntity T, taxConfig *productcatalog.TaxConfig) Base

patterns.name: goderive equality — never hand-edit derived.gen.go
patterns.description: Base.Equal delegates to deriveEqualBase; run go generate after adding fields. The generator uses .Equal() for decimal fields.
patterns.example: func (l Base) Equal(other Base) bool { return deriveEqualBase(&l, &other) }

patterns.name: Deep clone of pointer/slice fields
patterns.description: Base.Clone deep-copies TaxConfig pointer and CreditsApplied slice; new pointer/slice fields must be deep-copied to avoid aliasing.

patterns.name: Enum Values()/Validate() with assertion
patterns.description: Category implements models.Validator via slices.Contains against Values(); new enums follow the same pattern with var _ models.Validator = (*T)(nil).

key_file_guides.role: Base struct, Category enum, Comparable interface, Compare, BackfillTaxConfig.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a Base field requires updating Clone, Equal (goderive regen), DBGetter, Creator[T], FromDB, and mixin.go Fields() — all six together.

key_file_guides.role: Ent mixin providing all DB fields, SQL CHECK constraints, and indexes.
key_file_guides.watch_for: child_unique_reference_id has a NOT EMPTY CHECK — never make it optional. credits_applied is jsonb-optional with a pointer DB getter.

key_file_guides.role: Generic Create[T] populating Ent create mutations from a Base.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must stay in sync with all Base fields; a missing field silently omits it from writes. time.Time values use .In(time.UTC).

key_file_guides.role: DBGetter interface and FromDB generic mapping.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Apply time.UTC normalization to all time fields. GetCreditsApplied returns a pointer — dereference with lo.FromPtr.
After adding decimal fields, verify regenerated code uses .Equal() not ==.
Run after any Base change to regenerate derived.gen.go.

anti_patterns: Hand-editing derived.gen.go — always regenerate via go generate.
Adding DB/Ent logic to model.go or domain logic to mixin.go.
Forgetting to update Creator[T] and DBGetter when adding a Base field.
Making child_unique_reference_id optional — it has a DB CHECK and is the line upsert identity.

decisions.decision: Base struct co-located with the Ent mixin rather than a separate domain package.
decisions.rationale: Ent schema and domain model must stay lock-step; co-location makes drift visible and lets Creator/DBGetter share package types.

decisions.decision: goderive for equality rather than hand-written Equal.
decisions.rationale: Base has alpacadecimal.Decimal fields needing .Equal() not ==; goderive generates correct field-by-field equality kept in sync via go generate.

code_examples.scenario: Add a string field to Base and wire it through all layers

openmeter/billing/models/totals.purpose: Defines the Totals struct — a billing line's financial breakdown (amount, taxes, charges, discounts, credits, total) with currency-precision rounding, additive aggregation (Add/Sum), subtraction, negation, and an Ent mixin persisting all 8 fields as Postgres numeric columns. Used by all billing line types and invoice aggregations.

patterns.name: Ent mixin with typed Setter[T]/TotalsGetter
patterns.description: totals.Mixin declares all 8 numeric fields; use Set[T](mut, totals) and FromDB(e) for Ent interactions, never individual Set*/Get* in adapters.
patterns.example: func Set[T Setter[T]](mut Setter[T], totals Totals) T { return mut.SetAmount(totals.Amount).SetTaxesTotal(totals.TaxesTotal) /* all 8 */ }

patterns.name: RoundToPrecision before persist/compare
patterns.description: RoundToPrecision applies calculator.RoundToPrecision to every field atomically, returning a new Totals; always call before persisting or cross-currency comparison.

patterns.name: Additive aggregation via Add/Sum
patterns.description: Totals.Add accumulates field-by-field; Sum(...) aggregates a variadic list. Never accumulate individual fields outside these helpers.

patterns.name: Non-negative invariant in Validate
patterns.description: All 8 fields must be non-negative; new fields need a non-negative check in Validate.
patterns.example: if t.Amount.IsNegative() { return errors.New("amount is negative") }

patterns.name: Fixed CalculateTotal formula
patterns.description: CalculateTotal = Amount + ChargesTotal + TaxesExclusiveTotal - DiscountsTotal - CreditsTotal; never recompute inline.
patterns.example: func (t Totals) CalculateTotal() alpacadecimal.Decimal { return alpacadecimal.Sum(t.Amount, t.ChargesTotal, t.TaxesExclusiveTotal, t.DiscountsTotal.Neg(), t.CreditsTotal.Neg()) }

key_file_guides.role: Totals struct with Validate, Add, Sub, Neg, Sum, Equal, IsZero, RoundToPrecision, CalculateTotal.
key_file_guides.watch_for: A new field requires updating Add, Sub, Neg, RoundToPrecision, Equal, IsZero, Validate, and CalculateTotal (if in the total formula).

key_file_guides.role: Ent mixin + Setter[T]/TotalsGetter interfaces + Set[T]/FromDB helpers.
key_file_guides.watch_for: All fields use dialect.Postgres 'numeric'. A new field updates Fields(), Setter[T], TotalsGetter, Set[T], FromDB in lockstep — all five.

key_file_guides.role: Unit tests for RoundToPrecision (USD half-up) and Equal correctness.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Tests use exact string comparison on rounded decimals — update expected values if precision logic changes.

anti_patterns: Calling individual Set*/Get* methods for totals fields in adapters — use Set[T]/FromDB.
Adding a field without updating all of Add/Sub/Neg/Equal/IsZero/RoundToPrecision/Validate/mixin Fields()/Setter[T]/TotalsGetter/Set[T]/FromDB.

decisions.decision: All totals fields use alpacadecimal.Decimal stored as Postgres numeric.
decisions.rationale: Billing requires exact decimal arithmetic; float64 SQL types would introduce storage rounding errors.

decisions.decision: Mixin and domain model co-located in one package.
decisions.rationale: Keeps Setter/Getter interfaces in sync with the struct fields — a field addition immediately surfaces missing interface methods.
